question: Write a C++ program that initializes an array of 5 integers (e.g., {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}), then reverses the order of its elements *in-place* (without using a second array) and prints the modified array.
driver: valid C++ code to run test cases
errors: Inside the 'reverseArray' function, 'sizeof(arr)' is used on a function parameter 'arr' which has decayed from an array to a pointer ('int*'). This results in 'sizeof(arr)' returning the size of the pointer itself (e.g., 8 bytes on a 64-bit system) instead of the size of the original array. Consequently, 'n_incorrect' is calculated incorrectly (e.g., 2 instead of 5), leading to only a partial reversal of the array.
